entries.title: Decagon Area
entries.content: The area of a regular ten-sided polygon of side length s.
entries.example: A regular decagon with side length 3

entries.title: Decimal Conversion
entries.content: Converting a number from fractional or binary notation to base-10 decimal representation.
entries.example: Binary 10.1 equals decimal 2.5

entries.title: Decision Tree Probability
entries.content: A tree-like graph model of decisions and their possible consequences, used to calculate expected values.
entries.example: Deciding whether to fund a project with various success rates

entries.title: Decreasing Function
entries.content: A function whose output values decrease or remain the same as the input values increase.
entries.example: f(x) = -2x is strictly decreasing

entries.title: Definite Integral
entries.content: The integral of a function over a closed interval, representing the signed area under the curve.
entries.formula: Area = integral from a to b of f(x) dx
entries.example: Integral of x from 0 to 2 is 2

entries.title: Degree of Polynomial
entries.content: The highest power of the variable in a polynomial expression.
entries.example: Degree of 4x^3 - 2x + 7 is 3

entries.title: Degree of Vertex
entries.content: The number of edges connected to a vertex in a graph.
entries.example: In a square graph, each corner vertex has degree 2

entries.title: De Moivre's Theorem
entries.content: A formula that expresses complex powers of complex numbers in polar coordinates.
entries.example: Finding powers of (1 + i)

entries.title: De Morgan's Laws
entries.content: A pair of transformation rules in boolean algebra and set theory relating conjunction and disjunction through negation.
entries.formula: NOT (A AND B) = (NOT A) OR (NOT B)
entries.example: Complement of A union B is A' intersect B'

entries.title: Denominator
entries.content: The bottom number in a fraction, indicating the number of equal parts into which the whole is divided.
entries.example: In 3/4, the denominator is 4

entries.title: Dense Set
entries.content: A subset S of a topological space X is dense if its closure is equal to X.
entries.example: Rational numbers are dense in the real numbers

entries.title: Derivative Definition
entries.content: The limit representing the instantaneous rate of change of a function with respect to its variable.
entries.example: Derivative of x^2 is 2x

entries.title: Determinant of Matrix
entries.content: A scalar value calculated from a square matrix that encodes scaling and invertibility properties.

entries.title: Deviation from Mean
entries.content: The difference between an observed data value and the mean of the data set.
entries.example: Deviation of 8 from mean 6 is +2

entries.title: Diagonal Matrix
entries.content: A square matrix in which all entries outside the main diagonal are zero.
entries.example: Matrix with elements [2, 0, 0], [0, 5, 0], [0, 0, -1]

entries.title: Diameter of Circle
entries.content: The straight line distance passing through the center of a circle, connecting two opposite points.
entries.example: Diameter of circle with radius 7 is 14

entries.title: Difference of Squares
entries.content: An algebraic factoring pattern for the difference of two perfect squares.

entries.title: Difference Quotient
entries.content: The ratio of the difference of two values of a function to the difference of the corresponding inputs.
entries.example: Calculating slope of secant line

entries.title: Differentiability
entries.content: A function is differentiable at a point if its derivative exists at that point.
entries.example: f(x) = |x| is not differentiable at x = 0

entries.title: Differential Equation
entries.content: An equation relating a function to its derivatives.
entries.example: Equation for exponential growth of bacteria

entries.title: Digraph
entries.content: A directed graph where edges have a specific direction associated with them.
entries.formula: G = (V, E) with ordered pairs in E
entries.example: Flowchart showing work steps

entries.title: Dihedral Group
entries.content: The group of symmetries of a regular polygon, including rotations and reflections.
entries.example: Symmetries of an equilateral triangle (D_3, order 6)

entries.title: Dimension of Vector Space
entries.content: The number of vectors in a basis of a vector space.
entries.example: The dimension of R^3 is 3

entries.title: Diophantine Equation
entries.content: A polynomial equation in which only integer solutions are sought.
entries.example: Finding integer solutions for 3x + 5y = 12

entries.title: Direct Variation
entries.content: A relationship between two variables in which one is a constant multiple of the other.
entries.example: Distance traveled varies directly with time at constant speed

entries.title: Directrix of Parabola
entries.content: A fixed line used in the geometric definition of a parabola, opposite to the focus.
entries.example: Directrix of y = x^2 is y = -1/4

entries.title: Dirichlet's Principle
entries.content: Also known as the pigeonhole principle: if n items are put into m containers with n > m, at least one container has >1 item.
entries.formula: If N > M, exists box with >= 2 items
entries.example: In a group of 13 people, at least two were born in the same month

entries.title: Discrete Fourier Transform
entries.content: A transform that converts a finite sequence of equally-spaced samples of a signal into the frequency domain.
entries.example: Analyzing digital audio frequency components

entries.title: Discrete Random Variable
entries.content: A random variable that can take on only a countable number of distinct values.
entries.example: Number of heads in 5 coin flips

entries.title: Discriminant of Quadratic
entries.content: The part of the quadratic formula under the square root that reveals the type of roots.
entries.example: Discriminant of x^2 - 4x + 4 is 0 (one real root)

entries.title: Disjoint Sets
entries.content: Sets that have no elements in common, meaning their intersection is empty.
entries.example: Set of evens and set of odds are disjoint

entries.title: Disjunction in Logic
entries.content: An 'OR' logical operator which is true if at least one of the propositions is true.
entries.example: Statement: 'It is raining OR I have an umbrella'

entries.title: Distance Formula in 2D
entries.content: Formula calculating the straight line distance between two points in a Cartesian plane.
entries.example: Distance between (0,0) and (3,4) is 5

entries.title: Distance Formula in 3D
entries.content: Formula calculating the straight line distance between two points in three-dimensional space.
entries.example: Distance between (0,0,0) and (1,2,2) is 3

entries.title: Distributive Property
entries.content: Multiplying a sum by a number gives the same result as multiplying each addend individually and adding them.

entries.title: Divergence of Vector Field
entries.content: A vector operator that measures the magnitude of a field's source or sink at a given point.
entries.example: Calculating water flow outward from a sprinkler

entries.title: Divergence Test for Series
entries.content: A test showing that if the limit of the terms of a series is not zero, the series must diverge.
entries.example: Series 1 + 1 + 1 + ... diverges because lim(1) = 1 != 0

entries.title: Dividend and Divisor
entries.content: In division, the dividend is the number being divided, and the divisor is the number by which it is divided.
entries.example: In 15 / 3 = 5, 15 is dividend and 3 is divisor

entries.title: Divisibility Rules
entries.content: Shortcuts to quickly determine if an integer is divisible by another without full division.
entries.example: 123 is divisible by 3 because 1+2+3=6

entries.title: Domain of Function
entries.content: The complete set of all possible input values for which a function is defined and outputs real values.
entries.example: Domain of f(x) = 1/x is all x != 0
